title: Systems and methods for obtaining a smart panoramic image
abstract: Mobile handheld electronic devices such as smartphones, comprising a Wide camera for capturing Wide images with respective Wide fields of view (FOVW), a Tele camera for capturing Tele images with respective Tele fields of view (FOVT) smaller than FOVW, and a processor configured to stitch a plurality of Wide images into a panorama image with a field of view FOVP > FOVW and to pin a Tele image to a given location within the panorama image to obtain a smart panorama image.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A handheld device, comprising: a Wide camera for capturing Wide images, each Wide image having a respective Wide field of view (FOVw); a Tele camera for capturing Tele images, each Tele image having a respective Tele field of view (FOV T ) smaller than FOVw; and a processor configured to stitch a plurality of Wide images with respective FOVw into a panorama image with a field of view FOVp > FOVw and to pin a Tele image to a given location within the panorama image to obtain a smart panorama image. 2. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein each Wide image includes Wide scene information that is different from scene information of other Wide images. 3. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the processor is configured to crop the Tele image before pinning it to the given location. 4. The handheld device of claim 3, wherein the Tele images are cropped according to aesthetic criteria. 5. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the Wide camera is configured to capture Wide images autonomously. 6. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the Tele camera is configured to capture the Tele images autonomously. 7. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the processor is configured to use a motion model that predicts a future movement of the handheld device. 8. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the processor is configured to use a motion model that predicts a future movement of an object within the FOVp. 9. The handheld device of claim 6, wherein the processor is configured to use particular capture strategies for the autonomous capturing of the Tele images. 10. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the pinning a Tele image to a given location within the panorama image is obtained by executing localization between the Wide images and Tele images. 11. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the pinning a Tele image to a given location within the panorama image is obtained by executing localization between the panorama image and Tele images. 12. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the Tele camera has a plurality of zoom states. 13. The handheld device of claim 12, wherein the processor is configured to autonomously select a particular zoom state from the plurality of zoom states. 14. The handheld device of claim 12, wherein a particular zoom state from the plurality of zoom states is selected by a human user. 15. The handheld device of claim 12, wherein the plurality of zoom states includes a discrete number. 16. The handheld device of claim 12, wherein at least one of the plurality of zoom states can be modified continuously. 17. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the Tele camera is a scanning Tele camera. 18. The handheld device of claim 17, wherein the processor is configured to autonomously direct scanning of the FOV T to a specific location within a scene. 19. The handheld device of claim 17, wherein the FOV T scanning is performed by rotating one optical path folding element. 20. The handheld device of claim 17, wherein the FOV T scanning is performed by rotating two or more optical path folding elements. 21. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein each Tele image includes scene information from a center of the panorama image. 22. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein scene information in the Tele images includes scene information from a field of view larger than a native Tele field of view and smaller than a Wide field of view. 23. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein a particular segment of a scene is captured by the Tele camera and is pinned to locations within the panorama image. 24. The handheld device of claim 23, wherein the processor uses a tracking algorithm to capture the particular segment of a scene with the Tele camera. 25. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein a program decides which scene information captured by the Tele camera and pinned to locations within the panorama image. 26. The handheld device of claim 6, wherein the processor is configured to calculate a saliency map based on Wide image data to decide which scene information is captured by the Tele camera and pinned to locations within the panorama image. 27. The handheld device of claim 6, wherein the processor is configured to use a tracking algorithm to capture scene information with the Tele camera. 28. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the Tele image pinned to a given location within the panorama image is shown additionally in another location within the panorama image. 29. The handheld device of claim 1, wherein the Tele image pinned to a given location within the panorama image is shown in an enlarged scale. 30. A method, comprising: providing a plurality of Wide images, each Wide image having a respective Wide field of view (FOVw) and including Wide scene information different from other Wide images; providing a plurality of Tele images, each Tele image having a respective Tele field of view (FOV T ) that is smaller than FOVw; using a processor for stitching a plurality of Wide images into a panorama image with a panorama field of view FOVp > FOVw; and using the processor to pin at least one Tele image to a given location within the panorama image. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the handheld device is manually moved by a user to capture scene information in the FOVp.
